name: French Apple Pie
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare the crust and roll out two-thirds of the pastry to form a 12-inch circle.
2. Use it to line a 9-inch pie plate; refrigerate the rest of the pastry.
3. To make the filling: In a small saucepan, combine one-third cup sugar and the flour; mix well.
4. Stir in milk. Bring to boiling, stirring; reduce heat; simmer until slightly thickened (1 minute).
5. In a small bowl, beat three egg yolks slightly. Beat in some of the hot mixture; pour back into the saucepan, beating to mix well.
6. Stir in one tablespoon butter and the vanilla. Turn into a bowl to cool.
7. Core, pare, and slice apples; sprinkle with lemon juice.
8. In a medium skillet, heat butter with sugar and nutmeg. Add apple slices; sauté, stirring occasionally, until partially cooked (about 5 minutes).
9. Remove from heat. Heat apricot preserves just until melted.
10. Preheat the oven to 425°F.
11. Turn filling into pie shell, spreading evenly. Arrange apple slices on top, mounding slightly in the center.
12. Spread with apricot preserves.
13. Roll out the rest of the pastry to form a 10-inch circle. With a knife or pastry wheel, cut into 12 strips about half an inch wide.
14. Slightly moisten the rim of the pie shell with cold water. Arrange six strips across the filling; press ends to the rim of the pie shell, trimming off ends if necessary.
15. Arrange the rest of the strips at right angles to the first strips, forming a lattice. Bring the overhang of pastry up over the ends of the strips; crimp the edge.
16. Mix yolk with a tablespoon of water; use to brush the lattice strips, not the edge.
17. On the lowest shelf of the oven, bake for 35-40 minutes, or until the pastry is golden. Cool and serve slightly warm.
